summary: Verified ADR 0381 from the latest realistic mainline release context by replaying the Keycloak, shared-edge publication, and identity-core watchdog converge order on production, revalidating the ops/tasks login redirect contract end to end, and then repairing the remaining repository automation drift so the canonical status, receipt, and generated-doc validation paths all pass on current main.

verification.check: Integrated repo automation and validation slice
verification.observed: The final integrated validation set passed on current main: the focused pytest surface returned `142 passed` from `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-12-ws-0381-mainline-pytest-r1.txt`; `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards generated-docs generated-portals` plus `git diff --check` passed from `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-12-ws-0381-mainline-repo-gates-r1.txt`; and `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/live_apply_receipts.py --validate` passed from `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-12-ws-0381-mainline-receipts-validate-r1.txt` after refreshing canonical truth, generated status docs, the dependency diagram, and the Excalidraw-derived diagrams.

`make preflight WORKFLOW=converge-keycloak` and `make converge-keycloak env=production` both passed. The converge recap in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-12-ws-0381-main-converge-keycloak-0.178.126.txt` recorded `monitoring ok=20 changed=0 failed=0`, `nginx ok=49 changed=5 failed=0`, `postgres ok=80 changed=1 failed=0`, `proxmox-host ok=366 changed=0 failed=0`, and `runtime-control ok=290 changed=2 failed=0`.

verification.check: Exact-main public edge publication and certificate sweep
verification.observed: The first `make configure-edge-publication env=production` run stopped in the generated portal stage because the shared service catalog still referenced the unknown uptime monitor `Neko Public Health`. `make generate-uptime-kuma-monitors` repaired that shared input, the immediate rerun completed successfully, and the resulting evidence in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-12-ws-0381-main-configure-edge-publication-r2-0.178.126.txt` includes a successful `make validate-certificates` pass for 46 domains plus one unrelated `vault.lv3.org` connection reset against `100.64.0.1:443` outside ADR 0381.

`make preflight WORKFLOW=converge-identity-core-watchdog` and `make converge-identity-core-watchdog env=production` both passed, with `runtime-control ok=31 changed=0 failed=0` in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-12-ws-0381-main-converge-identity-core-watchdog-0.178.126.txt`.

`receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-12-ws-0381-main-auth-verification-r1.txt` shows `https://ops.lv3.org/` and `https://tasks.lv3.org/` returning `302` to `/oauth2/sign_in`, and both sign-in entrypoints returning `302` into the shared Keycloak authorize flow with `client_id=ops-portal-oauth`.

verification.check: Live watchdog and stale-session reset contract
verification.observed: The same auth-verification transcript confirms `lv3-ops-portal-oauth2-proxy-watchdog.timer` and `lv3-identity-watchdog.timer` active, `grep -c _lv3_ops_portal_proxy_csrf /etc/nginx/sites-available/lv3-edge.conf` returning `16`, `grep -c @oauth2_stale_session_reset ...` returning `32`, and `grep -c oauth2/sign_in\&client_id ...` returning `0` on the live edge host.

notes: The earlier branch receipt `2026-04-12-adr-0381-login-service-contracts-live-apply` remains the first-live proof from the isolated worktree; this mainline receipt is the canonical integrated evidence for repo version `0.178.126` and platform version `0.178.78`.

The exact-main auth replay was executed against the current local mainline series rooted at `18dfb4ea01cd6c3787a61eaa5cceff7cfd496543`, after verifying `origin/main` still trailed at `b2a4e23156ae0b4d7435a26095b3d3cdd3cab743`. The later main-only integration commit in this session repairs repo validation and generated-document drift without changing the live ADR 0381 auth surfaces.

Besides the unrelated `vault.lv3.org` certificate reset, the only runtime-adjacent blocker encountered during the exact-main replay was the shared uptime-monitor catalog drift that prevented the first `configure-edge-publication` pass; regenerating the uptime monitors cleared that shared input immediately.
